articles.title: Chitradurga heatwave intensifies in Karnataka - The News Mill
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Karnataka
articles.full_text:
Chitradurga in central Karnataka is experiencing a significant rise in temperatures, with residents struggling against intense heat, hot winds, and strong sunlight that have disrupted everyday life across the district.
Known for its historic forts and rocky terrain, the area is enduring unusually severe weather conditions this season. Locals report that the heat is becoming increasingly hard to tolerate during peak daytime hours.
Many residents avoid venturing outdoors between 10 am and 4 pm due to the extreme temperatures. There have been reports of dehydration, dizziness, and fatigue, particularly affecting students, college-goers, and tourists visiting local forts.
People have been relying heavily on water, buttermilk, and fruit juices to manage the heat, with growing concerns about the worsening conditions and their effects on daily life.
Sheema Kausher, Associate Professor at Government Science College, Chitradurga, told ANI, “the temperature levels this year are notably higher compared to previous years, making outdoor conditions more challenging.”
Ramesh Bhat, Associate Professor of Mathematics at the same institution, added, “people are facing significant discomfort due to the scorching heat, particularly in fort and rocky areas where temperatures feel even more intense.”
He also noted that the lack of rainfall has exacerbated the situation, raising health concerns and compelling residents to remain indoors during the hottest hours.
Locals expressed apprehension that if the current trend persists, the coming days may become increasingly difficult, with rising temperatures expected to further affect daily activities and outdoor movement.
Authorities and residents are urging caution as the region continues to endure ongoing heatwave-like conditions.

articles.title: Karnataka: Chitradurga heatwave intensifies, residents battle scorching Sun - lokmattimes.com
articles.full_text:
Karnataka: Chitradurga heatwave intensifies, residents battle scorching Sun
By ANI | Updated: April 26, 2026 02:20 IST2026-04-26T07:47:34+5:302026-04-26T02:20:03+5:30
Chitradurga (Karnataka) [India], April 26 : Chitradurga in central Karnataka is experiencing a steady rise in temperatures, with residents ...
Chitradurga (Karnataka) [India], April 26 : Chitradurga in central Karnataka is experiencing a steady rise in temperatures, with residents grappling with intense heat, hot winds and harsh sunlight that have disrupted normal daily life across the region.
Known for its historic forts and rocky landscape, the district is witnessing unusually severe weather conditions this season, with locals saying the heat has become increasingly difficult to bear during peak daytime hours.
Residents said that many people are avoiding stepping out between 10 am and 4 pm due to extreme heat conditions. Cases of dehydration, dizziness and fatigue have also been reported, particularly among students, college-goers and tourists visiting the forts.
Locals said that the rising temperatures have forced them to rely heavily on water, buttermilk and fruit juices to cope with the heat. Many expressed concern over the worsening situation and the impact it is having on daily activities.
Speaking to ANI, Sheema Kausher, Associate Professor at Government Science College, Chitradurga, said that the temperature levels this year are notably higher compared to previous years, making outdoor conditions more challenging.
Another Associate Professor of Mathematics from Government Science College, Ramesh Bhat, said that people are facing significant discomfort due to the scorching heat, particularly in fort and rocky areas where temperatures feel even more intense.
He added that the lack of rainfall has further aggravated the situation, causing health concerns among residents and forcing them to remain indoors during peak heat hours.
Locals also expressed worry that if the current trend continues, the coming days could become even more difficult, with rising temperatures expected to further impact daily life and outdoor movement.
Authorities and residents alike are now urging caution as the region continues to battle the ongoing heatwave-like conditions.
